text: When setting project goals, it is essential to use the SMART framework. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "finish the project soon," you could say "complete the market research report by April 15th." Breaking the project into smaller milestones—such as weekly targets—makes progress easier to track. Each milestone should lead to clear deliverables, like a prototype or a presentation. Also, ensure stakeholder alignment early on by discussing goals with everyone involved. This helps avoid misunderstandings and keeps the team moving in the same direction toward success.
